sentences.grammarNote: 一下 (yīxià) after a verb softens a request and makes it sound brief or casual: 请介绍一下 = 'please give a brief introduction'. It's equivalent to 'just' or 'a bit' in polite requests.

sentences.base_language: I graduated from Beijing University two years ago.
sentences.target_language: 我两年前从北京大学毕业了。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ liǎng nián qián cóng Běijīng dàxué bìyè le.
sentences.literal: I two years ago from Beijing University graduated.
sentences.grammarNote: 从 (cóng) = 'from (a starting point)'. Used for places of origin or starting points: 从学校来 (come from school), 从北京大学毕业 (graduate from Peking University). 前 after a time = 'ago': 两年前 = two years ago.

sentences.base_language: I have three years of work experience.
sentences.romanized: Wǒ yǒu sān nián de gōngzuò jīngyàn.
sentences.literal: I have three years work experience.

sentences.target_language: 我最大的优点是团队合作。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ zuì dà de yōudiǎn shì tuánduì hézuò.
sentences.grammarNote: 最 (zuì) = superlative: 最大 (biggest), 最好 (best), 最难 (hardest). Place it before an adjective. 最 + adj + 的 + noun = 'the most [adj] [noun]'.

sentences.base_language: Why do you want to work here?
sentences.target_language: 你为什么想在这里工作？
sentences.romanized: Nǐ wèishénme xiǎng zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò?
sentences.literal: You why want at here work?
sentences.grammarNote: 为什么 (wèishénme) = 'why'. Question words in Chinese stay in their natural position in the sentence — 为什么 typically comes right after the subject: 你为什么…

sentences.base_language: I'm very interested in this company's mission.
sentences.target_language: 我对这家公司的使命非常感兴趣。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ duì zhè jiā gōngsī de shǐmìng fēicháng gǎn xìngqù.
sentences.literal: I toward this company's mission extremely feel interest.
sentences.grammarNote: 对…感兴趣 = 'be interested in…'. The pattern is: subject + 对 + thing + 感兴趣. 对 here is a preposition meaning 'toward / regarding'. 家 is the measure word for companies.

sentences.base_language: Do you have any questions for us?
sentences.target_language: 您有什么问题要问我们吗？
sentences.romanized: Nín yǒu shénme wèntí yào wèn wǒmen ma?
sentences.literal: You have any questions to ask us?

sentences.base_language: When can I expect to hear back?
sentences.target_language: 我什么时候能收到回复？
sentences.romanized: Wǒ shénme shíhòu néng shōudào huífù?
sentences.grammarNote: 什么时候 (shénme shíhòu) = 'when'. Like other question words, it stays in the position where the answer would go: 我什么时候能… (When can I…). 能 here expresses possibility.
